project_overview.project_development_objective: To strengthen Ethiopia's institutional capacity for disaster risk management, and flood risk management in selected basins
project_overview.beneficiaries: 1,045,000 people directly (635,000 from flood risk reduction, 410,000 from early warning systems); 33 million people indirectly

geographic_scope.fragile_or_conflict_affected_areas: partial - project uses conflict-sensitive targeting approach, excludes Tigray region and other conflict-affected areas

key_components.component_name: Strengthening Institutional and Coordination Capacity for DRM
key_components.description: Improve GoE DRM by strengthening institutional capacity of federal agencies and coordination between federal and decentralized levels
Invest in physical infrastructure to reduce flood risks, strengthen hydromet services and early warning systems, increase community preparedness
Allows reallocation of project funds to support emergency response and reconstruction when triggered
Ensure successful implementation including training, M&E, procurement, fiduciary management, environmental and social standards

implementation_arrangements.implementing_agency: Ministry of Water and Energy (lead), Ethiopian Disaster Risk Management Commission, Ethiopian Meteorology Institute
implementation_arrangements.coordination_mechanisms: Steering Committee chaired by MoWE head, co-chaired by EDRMC head; Project Management Unit under MoWE; Project Coordination Unit under EDRMC
implementation_arrangements.procurement_approach: World Bank Procurement Regulations for IPF; procurement capacity building and consultant support provided
implementation_arrangements.monitoring_and_evaluation: Biannual progress reports, semi-annual results framework review, midterm review, baseline and end evaluation surveys

identified_risks.risk_description: Unstable political situation, social unrest, Tigray conflict, competing priorities for EDRMC resources
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Framework approach for flexibility, remote monitoring, strategic communication, enhanced transparency, security risk assessment

identified_risks.risk_description: Limited progress in DRM policy implementation, frequent institutional changes, lack of legal framework
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Monitor DRM sector changes, provide technical advice, support policy reform through implementation

identified_risks.risk_description: Complexity of flood protection infrastructure design, lack of detailed data, multiple-purpose infrastructure challenges
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Sufficient attention during feasibility studies, data collection activities, technical assistance

identified_risks.risk_description: Limited World Bank project experience, capacity building needs for flood risk management
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Establish PMU and PCU, hire consultants, provide training and technical assistance

identified_risks.risk_description: Limited World Bank experience, budget delays, weak internal controls, procurement capacity gaps
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Detailed FM procedures in POM, staff assignment, capacity building, procurement consultants

identified_risks.risk_description: Physical investments risks, security context, limited implementation capacity, land acquisition impacts
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: ESMF, ESCP, Resettlement Framework, GRM, security risk assessment, capacity building

safeguards_and_social.grievance_redress_mechanism: yes - project-level GRM at federal, regional, and woreda levels with multiple channels
safeguards_and_social.gender_considerations: Gender-sensitive flood early warning systems, revised Gender Mainstreaming in DRM guideline, female expert capacity building, participatory activities

fcv_relevant_details.conflict_sensitivity: Conflict-sensitive geographic targeting excluding Tigray and conflict-affected areas; framework approach for flexibility; security risk assessment required
fcv_relevant_details.fragility_context: High levels of fragility, disaster risk, poverty, and climate vulnerability; intersectional risks; legacy of conflict in Horn of Africa region
fcv_relevant_details.security_considerations: Security risk assessment and management plan required before Component 2 disbursement; remote monitoring capabilities; enhanced implementation support
fcv_relevant_details.displacement_or_refugees: yes - climate-related disasters drive displacement (504,000 displaced by disasters in 2019, 290,000 by 2020 floods); project aims to reduce displacement drivers
